cot_prefix: Okay, let's check each sentence against the provided text. First, the question is asking which statement is NOT true. Let's go through the options: (A) His father did not support his work as a bartender. The text mentions that Bobby's father told him to stop the craziness and get a job because he had a wife and daughter. However, being a waiter is not the same as being a bartender. The text says he worked as a waiter in Hollywood. There's no mention of him being a bartender. Therefore, the statement about his father not supporting his work as a bartender is incorrect because the text doesn't say he was a bartender, so it's an incorrect statement, meaning (A) is NOT true. (B) Before he became an overnight success, his life experienced ups and downs. This is true as per the text. He struggled in New York, moved to Hollywood, had a hard time, then his brother was murdered, he wrote a play, and eventually got success with the film Crash. So, his life definitely had ups and downs before becoming successful. (C) His brother's death inspired his writing Half-Deserted Streets. This is true. The text says he started writing as a way to explore the pain of Hell's Kitchen after his brother's murder and that Half-Deserted Streets was based on his brother's killing.
